title: Tools for cleaning rectangular data
text: collection simple functions cleaning rectangular data. package allows detect, count replace values delete rows/columns according specific predicate. addition, provides tools check conditions return informative error messages.

title: Tools for cleaning rectangular data
text: can install released version arkhe CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("arkhe") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/arkhe")

title: Tools for cleaning rectangular data
code: ## Load the package library(arkhe) ## Create a matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    6    7    8    9    2 #> [2,]    8   10   NA    7    6 #> [3,]   NA    1   10    4    1 #> [4,]    7   10    7   10    3 #> [5,]   NA    9    4    3    1  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 0 1 1 0 1 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 3 5 4 5 5  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    6    7    8    9    2 #> [2,]    7   10    7   10    3 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    7    9    2 #> [2,]   10    7    6 #> [3,]    1    4    1 #> [4,]   10   10    3 #> [5,]    9    3    1  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    6    7    8    9    2 #> [2,]    8   10    0    7    6 #> [3,]    0    1   10    4    1 #> [4,]    7   10    7   10    3 #> [5,]    0    9    4    3    1

title: Tools for cleaning rectangular data

what: arkhe: Tools for cleaning rectangular data — arkhe-package

text: collection simple functions cleaning rectangular data. package allows detect, count replace values delete rows/columns according specific predicate. addition, provides tools check conditions return informative error messages.

title: arkhe: Tools for cleaning rectangular data — arkhe-package
text: arkhe uses following options() configure behavior: arkhe.verbose: logical scalar. R report extra information progress? Defaults TRUE.

title: arkhe: Tools for cleaning rectangular data — arkhe-package

text: Assign specific row/column column/row names

title: Assign a specific row/column to the column/row names — assign
code: assign_colnames(x, ...)  assign_rownames(x, ...)  # S4 method for data.frame assign_rownames(x, column, remove = TRUE)  # S4 method for data.frame assign_colnames(x, row, remove = TRUE)

title: Assign a specific row/column to the column/row names — assign
text: x data.frame. ... Currently used. column length-one numeric vector specifying column number become row names. remove logical scalar: specified row/column removed making column/row names? row length-one numeric vector specifying row number become column names.

title: Assign a specific row/column to the column/row names — assign
text: data.frame.

title: Assign a specific row/column to the column/row names — assign
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Bootstrap Estimation — bootstrap
text: Samples randomly elements object replacement.

title: Bootstrap Estimation — bootstrap
code: bootstrap(object, ...)  # S4 method for numeric bootstrap(object, do, n, ..., f = NULL)

title: Bootstrap Estimation — bootstrap
text: object numeric vector. ... Extra arguments passed . function takes object argument returns single numeric value. n non-negative integer giving number bootstrap replications. f function takes single numeric vector (result ) argument.

title: Bootstrap Estimation — bootstrap
text: f NULL, bootstrap() returns result f applied n values . f NULL, bootstrap() returns named numeric  vector following elements: original observed value applied object. mean bootstrap estimate mean . bias bootstrap estimate bias . error bootstrap estimate standard error .

title: Bootstrap Estimation — bootstrap
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Bootstrap Estimation — bootstrap
code: x <- rnorm(20)  ## Bootstrap bootstrap(x, do = mean, n = 100) #>     original         mean         bias        error  #>  0.196370710  0.188907995 -0.007462714  0.247070117   ## Estimate the 25th and 95th percentiles quant <- function(x) { quantile(x, probs = c(0.25, 0.75)) } bootstrap(x, n = 100, do = mean, f = quant) #>        25%        75%  #> 0.07778279 0.36415659   ## Jackknife jackknife(x, do = mean) # Sample mean #>  original      mean      bias     error  #> 0.1963707 0.1963707 0.0000000 0.2770545

what: Remove values according to a given predicate — compact
title: Remove values according to a given predicate — compact
text: Removes rows/columns array-like object according given predicate.

title: Remove values according to a given predicate — compact
code: compact(x, f, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,`function` compact(x, f, margin = 1, negate = FALSE, all = FALSE)

title: Remove values according to a given predicate — compact
text: x object (matrix data.frame). f predicate function. ... Currently used. margin vector giving subscripts function applied (1 indicates rows, 2 indicates columns). negate logical scalar: negation f used instead f? logical scalar. TRUE, rows/columns whose values meet condition defined f considered. FALSE (default), rows/columns least one value validates condition defined f considered.

title: Remove values according to a given predicate — compact
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Remove values according to a given predicate — compact
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    3    9    5   NA    6 #> [2,]   10    1    6    1    9 #> [3,]    8    2    1    8    2 #> [4,]    9    1    5   10    9 #> [5,]    1   10   NA    8   NA  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 1 0 0 0 2 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 5 5 4 4 4  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]   10    1    6    1    9 #> [2,]    8    2    1    8    2 #> [3,]    9    1    5   10    9 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    3    9 #> [2,]   10    1 #> [3,]    8    2 #> [4,]    9    1 #> [5,]    1   10  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    3    9    5    0    6 #> [2,]   10    1    6    1    9 #> [3,]    8    2    1    8    2 #> [4,]    9    1    5   10    9 #> [5,]    1   10    0    8    0

title: Conditions — conditions
text: throw_error() stops execution current expression executes error action. throw_warning() generates warning message. catch_conditions() catch_message() handles unusual conditions.

title: Conditions — conditions
code: throw_error(.subclass, message, call = NULL, ...)  throw_warning(.subclass, message, call = NULL, ...)  catch_message(expr)  catch_conditions(expr)

title: Conditions — conditions
text: .subclass character string specifying class message returned. message character string specifying message returned. call call. ... Extra arguments. expr expression evaluated.

title: Conditions — conditions
text: N. Frerebeau

what: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
title: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
text: Computes confidence interval mean desired level significance.

title: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
code: confidence(object, ...)  # S4 method for numeric confidence(object, level = 0.95, type = c("student", "normal"))

title: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
text: object numeric vector. ... Currently used. level length-one numeric vector giving confidence level. Must single number \(0\) \(1\). type character string giving type confidence interval returned. must one "student" (default) "normal". unambiguous substring can given.

title: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
text: length-two numeric vector giving lower upper confidence limits.

title: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Confidence Interval for a Mean — confidence
code: x <- seq(from = -4, to = 4, by = 0.01) y <- dnorm(x)  confidence(y, type = "student") #>     lower     upper  #> 0.1151118 0.1345606  confidence(y, type = "normal") #>     lower     upper  #> 0.1151265 0.1345459

what: Count values according to a given predicate — count
title: Count values according to a given predicate — count
text: Counts values rows/columns according given predicate.

title: Count values according to a given predicate — count
code: count(x, f, ...)  # S4 method for matrix,`function` count(x, f, margin = 1, negate = FALSE)  # S4 method for data.frame,`function` count(x, f, margin = 1, negate = FALSE)

title: Count values according to a given predicate — count
text: x object (matrix data.frame). f predicate function. ... Currently used. margin vector giving subscripts function applied (1 indicates rows, 2 indicates columns). negate logical scalar: negation f used instead f?

title: Count values according to a given predicate — count
text: numeric vector.

title: Count values according to a given predicate — count
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Count values according to a given predicate — count
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]   NA    5    9    7   10 #> [2,]    1    1    9    8    4 #> [3,]    9   10    6    3    6 #> [4,]   NA    8   10   NA    1 #> [5,]    6    8    2    7    1  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 1 0 0 2 0 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 3 5 5 4 5  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    1    1    9    8    4 #> [2,]    9   10    6    3    6 #> [3,]    6    8    2    7    1 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    5    9   10 #> [2,]    1    9    4 #> [3,]   10    6    6 #> [4,]    8   10    1 #> [5,]    8    2    1  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    0    5    9    7   10 #> [2,]    1    1    9    8    4 #> [3,]    9   10    6    3    6 #> [4,]    0    8   10    0    1 #> [5,]    6    8    2    7    1

what: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
title: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
text: Finds rows/columns array-like object according given predicate.

title: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
code: detect(x, f, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,`function` detect(x, f, margin = 1, negate = FALSE, all = FALSE)

title: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
text: x object (matrix data.frame). f predicate function. ... Currently used. margin vector giving subscripts function applied (1 indicates rows, 2 indicates columns). negate logical scalar: negation f used instead f? logical scalar. TRUE, rows/columns whose values meet condition defined f considered. FALSE (default), rows/columns least one value validates condition defined f considered.

title: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
text: logical vector.

title: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Find values according to a given predicate — detect
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    8    2    4    4    6 #> [2,]   NA   NA   NA    8    1 #> [3,]    5    8    7    3    7 #> [4,]    1    4    6    4    5 #> [5,]   10    9   10    1    4  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 0 3 0 0 0 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 4 4 4 5 5  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    8    2    4    4    6 #> [2,]    5    8    7    3    7 #> [3,]    1    4    6    4    5 #> [4,]   10    9   10    1    4 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    4    6 #> [2,]    8    1 #> [3,]    3    7 #> [4,]    4    5 #> [5,]    1    4  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    8    2    4    4    6 #> [2,]    0    0    0    8    1 #> [3,]    5    8    7    3    7 #> [4,]    1    4    6    4    5 #> [5,]   10    9   10    1    4

title: Empty Values — empty
text: remove_empty() remove empty rows/columns.

title: Empty Values — empty
code: remove_empty(x, ...)  remove_empty_cols(x, ...)  remove_empty_rows(x, ...)  # S4 method for ANY remove_empty(x, margin = 1)  # S4 method for ANY remove_empty_cols(x)  # S4 method for ANY remove_empty_rows(x)

title: Empty Values — empty
text: x object (matrix data.frame). ... Currently used. margin vector giving subscripts function applied (1 indicates rows, 2 indicates columns).

title: Empty Values — empty
text: row/column empty contains NA, zeros (type numeric) zero length character strings (type character).

title: Empty Values — empty
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Empty Values — empty
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    8    8    4   10    8 #> [2,]    7    2    1    7    8 #> [3,]    3    1    7    5    3 #> [4,]   NA    4    8    4   NA #> [5,]   NA    2    4    6    7  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 0 0 0 2 1 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 3 5 5 5 4  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    8    8    4   10    8 #> [2,]    7    2    1    7    8 #> [3,]    3    1    7    5    3 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    8    4   10 #> [2,]    2    1    7 #> [3,]    1    7    5 #> [4,]    4    8    4 #> [5,]    2    4    6  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    8    8    4   10    8 #> [2,]    7    2    1    7    8 #> [3,]    3    1    7    5    3 #> [4,]    0    4    8    4    0 #> [5,]    0    2    4    6    7

title: Infinite Values — infinite
text: remove_Inf() remove rows/columns contain infinite values. replace_Inf replaces infinite values values.

title: Infinite Values — infinite
code: remove_Inf(x, ...)  replace_Inf(x, ...)  # S4 method for ANY remove_Inf(x, margin = 1, all = FALSE)  # S4 method for matrix replace_Inf(x, value = 0)

title: Infinite Values — infinite
text: x object (matrix data.frame). ... Currently used. margin vector giving subscripts function applied (1 indicates rows, 2 indicates columns). logical scalar. TRUE, rows/columns whose values meet condition defined f considered. FALSE (default), rows/columns least one value validates condition defined f considered. value possible replacement value.

title: Infinite Values — infinite
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Infinite Values — infinite
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    9    8    9    6    8 #> [2,]    7    4    6   10    3 #> [3,]   NA    7    6    4    1 #> [4,]   NA    3    4    6    6 #> [5,]    5    7    4   NA    4  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 0 0 1 1 1 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 3 5 5 4 5  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    9    8    9    6    8 #> [2,]    7    4    6   10    3 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    8    9    8 #> [2,]    4    6    3 #> [3,]    7    6    1 #> [4,]    3    4    6 #> [5,]    7    4    4  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    9    8    9    6    8 #> [2,]    7    4    6   10    3 #> [3,]    0    7    6    4    1 #> [4,]    0    3    4    6    6 #> [5,]    5    7    4    0    4

title: Jackknife Estimation — jackknife
text: Jackknife Estimation

title: Jackknife Estimation — jackknife
code: jackknife(object, ...)  # S4 method for numeric jackknife(object, do, ...)

title: Jackknife Estimation — jackknife
text: object numeric vector. ... Extra arguments passed . function takes object argument returns single numeric value.

title: Jackknife Estimation — jackknife
text: Returns named numeric  vector following elements: original observed value applied object. mean jackknife estimate mean . bias jackknife estimate bias . error jackknife estimate standard error .

title: Jackknife Estimation — jackknife
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Jackknife Estimation — jackknife
code: x <- rnorm(20)  ## Bootstrap bootstrap(x, do = mean, n = 100) #>    original        mean        bias       error  #>  0.23657208  0.21806192 -0.01851016  0.20524044   ## Estimate the 25th and 95th percentiles quant <- function(x) { quantile(x, probs = c(0.25, 0.75)) } bootstrap(x, n = 100, do = mean, f = quant) #>        25%        75%  #> 0.09968239 0.40242058   ## Jackknife jackknife(x, do = mean) # Sample mean #>  original      mean      bias     error  #> 0.2365721 0.2365721 0.0000000 0.2279105

text: N. Frerebeau

title: Missing Values — missing
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    2    4    8    1    1 #> [2,]    6    7    5   NA    9 #> [3,]   NA    5    3    8    3 #> [4,]    7    4    9   NA    8 #> [5,]    1   10    9    2    4  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 0 1 1 1 0 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 4 5 5 3 5  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    2    4    8    1    1 #> [2,]    1   10    9    2    4 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    4    8    1 #> [2,]    7    5    9 #> [3,]    5    3    3 #> [4,]    4    9    8 #> [5,]   10    9    4  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    2    4    8    1    1 #> [2,]    6    7    5    0    9 #> [3,]    0    5    3    8    3 #> [4,]    7    4    9    0    8 #> [5,]    1   10    9    2    4

title: Matrix Predicates — predicate-matrix
text: is_square() checks matrix square. is_symmetric() checks matrix symmetric.

title: Matrix Predicates — predicate-matrix
code: is_square(x)  is_symmetric(x)

title: Matrix Predicates — predicate-matrix
text: x matrix tested.

title: Matrix Predicates — predicate-matrix
text: logical scalar.

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
text: Check numeric objects: is_zero() checks object contains zeros. is_odd() is_even() check number odd even, respectively. is_positive() is_negative check object contains (strictly) positive negative numbers. is_whole() checks object contains whole numbers.

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
code: is_zero(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_odd(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_even(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_positive(x, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_negative(x, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_whole(x, na.rm = FALSE, tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5)

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
text: x numeric object tested. na.rm logical scalar: missing values (including NaN) omitted? strict logical scalar: strict inequality used? tolerance numeric scalar giving tolerance check within.

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
text: logical vector.

title: Scalar Type Predicates — predicate-scalar
text: Scalar Type Predicates

title: Scalar Type Predicates — predicate-scalar
code: is_scalar_list(x)  is_scalar_atomic(x)  is_scalar_vector(x)  is_scalar_numeric(x)  is_scalar_integer(x)  is_scalar_double(x)  is_scalar_character(x)  is_scalar_logical(x)

title: Scalar Type Predicates — predicate-scalar
text: x object tested.

title: Scalar Type Predicates — predicate-scalar
text: logical scalar.

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
text: Check numeric objects: is_constant() checks equality among elements vector. is_increasing() is_decreasing() check sequence numbers monotonically increasing decreasing, respectively.

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
code: is_constant(x, tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5, na.rm = FALSE)  is_increasing(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_decreasing(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_greater(x, y, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_lower(x, y, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
text: x, y numeric object tested. tolerance numeric scalar giving tolerance check within. na.rm logical scalar: missing values (including NaN) omitted? strict logical scalar: strict inequality used?

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
text: logical scalar.

title: Type Predicates — predicate-type
text: Type Predicates

title: Type Predicates — predicate-type
code: is_list(x)  is_atomic(x)  is_vector(x)  is_numeric(x)  is_integer(x)  is_double(x)  is_character(x)  is_logical(x)  is_error(x)  is_warning(x)  is_message(x)

title: Type Predicates — predicate-type
text: x object tested.

title: Type Predicates — predicate-type
text: logical scalar.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
text: is_empty() checks object empty (zero-length dimensions). has_length() checks long object. has_names() checks object named. has_duplicates() checks object duplicated elements. has_missing() has_infinite() check object contains missing infinite values.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
code: has_length(x, n = NULL)  has_names(x, names = NULL)  has_duplicates(x)  has_missing(x)  has_infinite(x)  is_empty(x)

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
text: x vector tested. n length-one numeric vector specifying length test x . NULL, returns TRUE x length greater zero, FALSE otherwise. names character vector specifying names test x . NULL, returns TRUE x names, FALSE otherwise.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
text: logical scalar.

title: Reshape — reshape
text: Transforms matrix long data.frame.

title: Reshape — reshape
code: as_long(from, ...)  # S4 method for matrix as_long(from, factor = FALSE, reverse = FALSE)

title: Reshape — reshape
text: object coerced. ... Currently used. factor logical scalar: character string coerced factor? Default FALSE, TRUE original ordering preserved. reverse logical scalar: order factor levels reversed? used factor TRUE. Useful plotting.

title: Reshape — reshape
text: coerced object.

title: Reshape — reshape
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Reshape — reshape
code: ## Create a matrix A <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 20, ncol = 5)  ## Transform to long data.frame head(as_long(A)) #>   row column value #> 1   1      1     0 #> 2   2      1     5 #> 3   3      1     1 #> 4   4      1     1 #> 5   5      1     9 #> 6   6      1     1

text: Validate Condition

code: validate(expr)

text: expr object evaluated.

title: Validate a Condition — validate
text: Returns NULL success, otherwise returns error string.

title: Validate a Condition — validate
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Zeros — zero
text: remove_zero() remove rows/columns contain zeros. replace_zero replaces zeros.

title: Zeros — zero
code: remove_zero(x, ...)  replace_zero(x, ...)  # S4 method for ANY remove_zero(x, margin = 1, all = FALSE)  # S4 method for matrix replace_zero(x, value)

title: Zeros — zero
text: x object (matrix data.frame). ... Currently used. margin vector giving subscripts function applied (1 indicates rows, 2 indicates columns). logical scalar. TRUE, rows/columns whose values meet condition defined f considered. FALSE (default), rows/columns least one value validates condition defined f considered. value possible replacement value.

title: Zeros — zero
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Zeros — zero
code: ## Create a count data matrix X <- matrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)  ## Add NA k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE) X[k] <- NA X #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    4   10    4   NA    9 #> [2,]    3    9    5    2    1 #> [3,]    1    9    1    1   NA #> [4,]    3    1   10    9   NA #> [5,]   10    3    3    2    3  ## Count missing values in rows count(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1] 1 0 1 1 0 ## Count non-missing values in columns count(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE) #> [1] 5 5 5 4 3  ## Find row with NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 1) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE ## Find column without any NA detect(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, negate = TRUE, all = TRUE) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  ## Remove row with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 1, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    3    9    5    2    1 #> [2,]   10    3    3    2    3 ## Remove column with any NA compact(X, f = is.na, margin = 2, all = FALSE) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    4   10    4 #> [2,]    3    9    5 #> [3,]    1    9    1 #> [4,]    3    1   10 #> [5,]   10    3    3  ## Replace NA with zeros replace_NA(X, value = 0) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    4   10    4    0    9 #> [2,]    3    9    5    2    1 #> [3,]    1    9    1    1    0 #> [4,]    3    1   10    9    0 #> [5,]   10    3    3    2    3
